IT IS ALIGNED WITH GLOBAL PRIORITI

The sand battery can substitute petroleum products heating(kerosene,diesel,charcoal) and use green energy(solar) as primary heating source. Heated water is required in the hospitality(restaurants ,hotels) , learning institutions(Boarding schools, Universities) and Hospitals.
s need constant hot water – for showers, laundry, kitchens, and swimming pools. Most hotels use electric water heaters or diesel boilers.
s need hot water for hundreds of students. Kitchens need hot water for cooking and washing. Sanitation requires warm water for showers and cleaning. But schools operate on tight budgets.

s need hot water 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Laundry must be sanitized. Surgical instruments must be sterilized. Patients need warm water for hygiene. And the hot water cannot stop – ever.For hospitals there cannot be disruptions in the water supply so this can also act as backup.
Heating/charging is done during the day.At night when hot water is needed its withdrawn after passing through the heat exchangers for use.

The sand battery provides hot air in a wide temperature range. For drying fish and fruits 50-70 degrees. Green house heating 20-35 degrees. Poultry brooding or incubating 35 degrees. Agricultural drying 60-150 degrees for maize,coffee,tea e.t.c.
s need stable temperatures for optimal plant growth. At night, temperatures drop – damaging tomatoes, peppers, roses, and vegetables. Too cold means stunted growth, low yields, and crop loss.
s need constant warmth – 35 degrees Celsius for the first week. If the temperature drops, chicks stress, get sick, or die. Even a few hours of cold can kill hundreds of chicks.
s in the field. Coffee is sun-dried for days – then rain ruins it. Tea factories spend millions drying leaves with diesel.
Open sun drying is slow and unreliable.

Concentrated Solar Power (CSP) plants collect solar heat during the day – but without storage, they shut down at sunset. Today, most CSP plants use molten salt to store heat – but molten salt is expensive, corrosive, and freezes below 120°C. Our sand battery is a drop-in replacement: cheaper, safer, and more reliable. It stores heat at up to 450°C and delivers it when the sun isn't shining – generating steam for turbines during peak hours acting like a battery. In this case the molten salt acts as the working fluid. Oil can be used as the substitute to prevent cases of freezing.Sand is abundant, non-toxic, and costs a fraction of molten salt.
ELECTRIC GRID SUPPLY,PEAK LOAD MANAGEMENT,DATA CENTERS.

s underutilized because it's intermittent. Our sand battery makes CSP dispatchable – 24-hour renewable power at a fraction of the cost of batteries or diesel. Grid operators get reliable baseload power. Peak demand is met with clean energy. Data centers achieve 100% renewable operation.

Our sand battery is the perfect partner for Concentrated Solar Power in industrial heating. CSP mirrors concentrate sunlight to heat sand to 450°C – storing enough thermal energy to power factories 24 hours a day. No diesel. No electricity. Just free, abundant sunlight stored in sand. Cement plants can preheat raw materials overnight. Tea factories can dry leaves with solar heat around the clock. Dairy processors can pasteurize milk using clean, stored solar energy.Meat processers like Kenya Meat Commission dry meat for packaging. The result: industries eliminate emissions, and gain energy independence.

Renewable energy sources – solar and wind – are intermittent. The sun doesn't shine at night. The wind doesn't always blow. To build a 100% renewable grid, we need long-duration energy storage (LDES) – and our sand battery is a solution.
Our sand battery is the missing link for renewable energy. When

solar farms produce more power than the grid needs, we convert that excess electricity into heat and store it in sand at 450°C. When wind turbines spin at , we capture that power and store it. When the grid needs electricity – during peak hours or when the sun isn't shining – we convert stored heat back to power. Sand stores energy for days or weeks, at 90% lower cost than lithium batteries, with zero degradation over years. This is how we build a 100% renewable grid – not with expensive batteries, but with abundant, low-cost sand.
Heating/charging is done during the day. At night when power is needed steam is generated.
